Appropriation of Moneys in Advance as Requisite to Purchases, Investments, or Other Acquisitions of Equity by Fund Created Under Pilot Equity Finance Program, , , provided in part: , and further provided “That purchases, investments or other acquisitions of equity by the fund created by section 104 of S. 2757 as hereby enacted [former ] are limited to such amounts as may be provided in advance in appropriations Acts”.Pub. Ex. Ord. No. 11579. Overseas Private Investment CorporationEx. Ord. No. 11579, , 36 F.R. 969, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 12107, , 44 F.R. 1055; Ex. Ord. No. 12163, , 44 F.R. 56673, provided:Jan. 19, 1971Dec. 28, 1978Sept. 29, 1979By virtue of the authority vested in me by the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 (), as amended (hereinafter the “Act”) [section 2151 et seq. of this title] and of the United States Code, and as President of the United States, it is ordered as follows:75 Stat. 424section 301 of title 31. . All obligations, assets and related rights and responsibilities arising out of, or related to, predecessor programs and authorities similar to those provided for in sections 234(a), (b) and (d) of the Act [former section 2194(a), (b) and (d) of this title] are hereby transferred to the Overseas Private Investment Corporation (hereinafter the “Corporation”).SectionTransfer to Overseas Private Investment Corporation. 2. . (a) [Revoked by Ex. Ord. No. 12163, , 44 F.R. 56673.]SecDelegation of functionsSept. 29, 1979(b) The function of prescribing regulations relating to the reinstatement or restoration of officers and employees of the Corporation to other government positions, when their appointment to a position in the Corporation was made from another government position and their separation from the Corporation was not made for cause, is hereby delegated to the Office of Personnel Management.. 3. . Funds made available under section 232 of the Act (repealed by section 105 of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1969) [former ] which are obligated but unexpended are hereby transferred to the Corporation.SecAllocation and transfer of fundssection 2192 of this title. 4. . (a) As used in this order, the words “function” or “functions” include any duty, obligation, power, authority, responsibility, right, privilege, discretion, or activity.SecGeneral provisions(b) The Corporation shall be deemed to be the successor of the Agency for International Development and the Administrator thereof, with respect to all functions vested in the Corporation pursuant to law.(c) Except to the extent that they may be inconsistent with this order, all determinations, authorizations, regulations, rulings, certificates, orders, directives, contracts, agreements, and other actions made, issued, or entered into with respect to any function affected by this order and not revoked, superseded or otherwise made inapplicable before the date of this order, shall continue in full force and effect until amended, modified, or terminated by appropriate authority.(d) Executive Order No. 10973 of , as amended [formerly set out as a note under ], is hereby superseded insofar as any provision therein is in conflict with any provision herein.November 3, 1961section 2381 of this title(e) The provisions of this order shall become effective upon adoption by the Board of Directors of bylaws for the Corporation.[For transfer of functions, personnel, assets, and liabilities of the Overseas Private Investment Corporation to the United States International Development Finance Corporation and treatment of related references, see sections 9683 and 9686(d) of this title.]
